prose:
**Water frontier-orbital result** — Property: HOMO–LUMO gap. Method used: DFT tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied fixed XYZ file `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/water/water.xyz` (3 atoms); this was a frontier-orbitals/single-point calculation, not a geometry optimization. SCF converged in 7 cycles. No warnings were reported by the chemkit result.

Headline value: **HOMO–LUMO gap = 8.9406687 eV**.

Orbital energies returned by chemkit: HOMO = -8.6037183 eV; LUMO = 0.33695037 eV. Koopmans-style quantities returned: vertical IP = 8.6037183 eV; vertical EA = -0.33695037 eV.

Total electronic energy returned: -76.462901 hartree (-2080.6615 eV). Energy zero: electronic energy (bare nuclei + electrons).

Reproducibility: chemkit CLI invocation returned was `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 --solvent gas /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/water/water.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpl8e9p_fs/live.json`. The chemkit result did not provide a live `.out` log path in the returned JSON, so I am not inventing one.

Engine integrity verdict: `trustworthy: true`. Provenance: computed.
